title: Agent Pulse
description: Source-code motion for curated AI-agent and agent-adjacent projects covered by The Git Reporter.

scope.coverageStatement: V0.1 measures visible source-code motion, hardening-subject proxy signals, daily git trends, and The Git Reporter article coverage only. Community feedback metrics are absent until the GitHub feedback collector ships.

editorialRead.headline: Morning Read — July 10, 2026
editorialRead.readHeadline: earendil-works/pi is the first source to inspect.
editorialRead.oneLineSummary: earendil-works/pi leads a 3-item attention queue backed by direct receipts; 13 watchlist sources are fresh, 0 need source-health attention, and movement still requires editorial judgment.
editorialRead.dek: Agent Pulse filtered 13 included projects down to 3 items worth finite newsroom attention. The queue protects attention; it does not declare winners.
bullets: earendil-works/pi — 5 commits today and 1 same-day mapped article, 7.7 below its current daily pace, with 8 hardening-subject points. Inspect the receipt before assigning a story.
openclaw/openclaw — 83 commits landed today. Keep it on watch, but require the next receipt to prove a reader consequence before promoting it.
alejandro-ao/tau — No same-day commit after 83 commits in the seven-day window. The pause is notable enough to preserve, not enough to turn into a story.
editorialRead.watchNext: The next meaningful upgrade is feedback flow: issues opened and closed, review latency, stale backlog, and the share of work that starts outside the maintainer team.
editorialRead.caveat: Source-code motion and article coverage are collected. GitHub issue flow, PR review timing, community discussion, and private backlog signals are not.

attentionQueue.note: At most three projects survive the morning filter. A receipt is required; movement alone is not a story.
